words.def: an arrangement of the parts of something or a group of things; the form or shape that this arrangement produces
words.example: configuration of something, The design is based on four configurations of squares.

words.def: to keep somebody/something inside the limits of a particular activity, subject, area, etc.
words.example: be confined to (doing) something, The work will not be confined to the Glasgow area.

words.def: a statement, letter, etc. that shows that something is true, correct or definite
words.example: I'm still waiting for confirmation of the test results.

words.def: to appear and need to be dealt with by somebody
words.example: What is to be done about the economic problems confronting the country?

words.def: a situation in which there is anger between people or groups who disagree because they have different opinions
words.example: confrontation (with somebody), She wanted to avoid another confrontation with her father.

words.def: to tell somebody that you are pleased about their success or achievements
words.example: I congratulated them all on their results.

words.def: a group of people who are gathered together in a church for a religious service, not including the priest and choir
words.example: The congregation stood to sing the hymn.

words.def: related to or belonging to a congress or the Congress in the US
words.example: a congressional committee/bill

words.def: to take control of a country or city and its people by force
words.example: The Normans conquered England in 1066.

words.def: the part of your mind that tells you whether your actions are right or wrong
words.example: to have a clear/guilty conscience (= to feel that you have done right/wrong)

words.def: the state of being able to use your senses and mental powers to understand what is happening
words.example: I can't remember any more—I must have lost consciousness.

words.def: following one after another in a continuous series
words.example: She was absent for nine consecutive days.

words.def: an opinion that all members of a group agree with
words.example: consensus (about/on something), She is skilled at achieving consensus on sensitive issues.

words.def: permission to do something, especially given by somebody in authority
words.example: The written consent of a parent is required.

words.def: to protect something and prevent it from being changed or destroyed
words.example: new laws to conserve wildlife in the area

words.def: the quality of always behaving in the same way or of having the same opinions, standard, etc.; the quality of being consistent
words.example: She has played with great consistency all season.

words.def: to make a position of power or success stronger so that it is more likely to continue
words.example: With this new movie he has consolidated his position as the country's leading director.

words.def: a district that elects its own representative to parliament
words.example: Unemployment is high in her constituency.

words.def: to be considered to be something
words.example: Does such an activity constitute a criminal offence?

words.def: the system of laws and basic principles that a state, a country or an organization is governed by
words.example: your right to vote under the constitution

words.def: a thing that limits something, or limits your freedom to do something
words.example: constraints of time/money/space

words.def: the act of discussing something with somebody or with a group of people before making a decision about it
words.example: a consultation document/paper/period/process

words.def: to think about whether you should do something, or how you should do something
words.example: contemplate something, You're too young to be contemplating retirement.

words.def: the feeling that somebody/something is without value and deserves no respect at all
words.example: with contempt, She looked at him with contempt.

words.def: to say that something is true, especially in an argument
words.example: I would contend that the minister's thinking is flawed on this point.

words.def: a person or team with a chance of winning a competition
words.example: contender (for something), a contender for a gold medal in the Olympics

words.def: happy and satisfied with what you have
words.example: He seemed more content, less bitter.

words.def: anger between people who disagree
words.example: One area of contention is the availability of nursery care.

words.def: in a way that is repeated many times so that it is annoying
words.example: They argue continually about money.

words.def: a person or company that has a contract to do work or provide goods or services for another company
words.example: a building/roofing/electrical contractor

words.def: a lack of agreement between facts, opinions, actions, etc.
words.example: contradiction (between A and B), There is a contradiction between the two sets of figures.

words.def: the opposite fact, event or situation
words.example: In the end the contrary was proved true: he was innocent and she was guilty.

words.def: a person who writes articles for a magazine, book or website, or who talks on a radio or television programme or at a meeting
words.example: a regular contributor to this magazine

words.def: the act or process of changing something from one form, use or system to another
words.example: Their main business is the conversion of farm buildings into family homes.

words.def: to decide and state officially in court that somebody is guilty of a crime
words.example: be convicted (of something), He was convicted of fraud.

words.def: the act of finding somebody guilty of a crime in court; the fact of having been found guilty
words.example: He plans to appeal against his conviction.

words.def: to work together with somebody else in order to achieve something
words.example: The two groups agreed to cooperate with each other.

words.def: involving doing something together or working together with others towards a shared aim
words.example: Cooperative activity is essential to effective community work.

words.def: to organize the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
words.example: coordinate something, They appointed a new manager to coordinate the work of the team.

words.def: the act of making parts of something, groups of people, etc. work together in an efficient and organized way
words.example: The aim was to improve the coordination of services.

words.def: a person who organizes the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
words.example: The campaign needs an effective coordinator.

words.def: a police officer
words.example: Somebody call the cops!

words.def: a chemical element. Copper is a soft red-brown metal used for making electric wires, pipes and coins.
words.example: a copper mine

words.def: if a person or an organization holds the copyright on a piece of writing, music, etc., they are the only people who have the legal right to publish, broadcast, perform it, etc., and other people must ask their permission to use it or any part of it
words.example: Copyright expires seventy years after the death of the author.

words.def: a change that makes something more accurate than it was before
words.example: I've made a few small corrections to your report.

words.def: if two or more facts, figures, etc. correlate or if a fact, figure, etc. correlates with another, the facts are closely connected and affect or depend on each other
words.example: The figures do not seem to correlate.

words.def: a connection between two things in which one thing changes as the other does
words.example: correlation between A and B, There is a direct correlation between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

words.def: to be the same as or match something
words.example: Your account and hers do not correspond.

words.def: the letters, emails, etc. a person sends and receives
words.example: personal/private correspondence

words.def: a person who reports news from a particular country or on a particular subject for a newspaper or a television or radio station
words.example: She's the BBC's political correspondent.

words.def: matching or connected with something that you have just mentioned
words.example: A change in the money supply brings a corresponding change in expenditure.

words.def: willing to use their power to do dishonest or illegal things in return for money or to get an advantage
words.example: It was seen as the only way to overthrow a corrupt regime.

words.def: dishonest or illegal behaviour, especially of people in authority
words.example: allegations of bribery and corruption

words.def: costing a lot of money, especially more than you want to pay
words.example: Buying new furniture may prove too costly.

words.def: a member of a city or county council
words.example: Councillor Ann Jones

words.def: professional advice about a problem
words.example: The couple decided to go for relationship counselling.

words.def: a person who has been trained to advise people with problems, especially personal problems
words.example: I went to see a debt counsellor and she agreed to come to the bank with me.

words.def: a long flat surface over which goods are sold or business is done in a shop, bank, etc.
words.example: behind the counter, I asked the woman behind the counter if they had any postcards.

words.def: a person or thing that has the same position or function as somebody/something else in a different place or situation
words.example: The Foreign Minister held talks with his Chinese counterpart.

words.def: very many; too many to be counted or mentioned
words.example: I've warned her countless times.

words.def: a sudden change of government that is illegal and often violent
words.example: He seized power in a military coup in 2008.

words.def: polite behaviour that shows respect for other people
words.example: I was treated with the utmost courtesy by the staff.

words.def: an activity involving a special skill at making things with your hands
words.example: traditional crafts like basket-weaving

words.def: to move forward on your hands and knees or with your body close to the ground
words.example: Our baby is just starting to crawl.

words.def: a person who has made or invented a particular thing
words.example: Walt Disney, the creator of Mickey Mouse

words.def: the quality that somebody/something has that makes people believe or trust them
words.example: to gain/lack/lose credibility

words.def: that can be believed or trusted
words.example: a credible explanation/witness

words.def: to move slowly, quietly and carefully, because you do not want to be seen or heard
words.example: I crept up the stairs, trying not to wake my parents.

words.def: a piece of written criticism of a set of ideas, a work of art, etc.
words.example: She wrote a feminist critique of Freud's theories.

words.def: an object in the shape of a circle, usually made of gold and precious stones, that a king or queen wears on his or her head on official occasions
words.example: The crown was placed upon the new monarch's head.

words.def: in its natural state, before it has been processed or refined
words.example: crude oil/metal

words.def: to press something so hard that it is damaged or injured, or loses its shape
words.example: The car was completely crushed under the truck.

words.def: a small piece of a substance with many even sides, that is formed naturally when the substance becomes solid
words.example: ice/salt crystals

words.def: very popular with a particular group of people
words.example: a cult movie/book

words.def: to prepare and use land for growing plants or crops
words.example: The land around here has never been cultivated.

words.def: a strong desire to know about something
words.example: Children show curiosity about everything.

words.def: the legal right or duty to take care of or keep somebody/something; the act of taking care of something/somebody
words.example: Who will have custody of the children?

words.def: an article or a story that you cut from a newspaper or magazine and keep
words.example: newspaper/press cuttings

words.def: believing that people only do things to help themselves rather than for good or honest reasons
words.example: a cynical view/smile

words.def: a barrier that is built across a river in order to stop the water from flowing, used especially to make a reservoir (= a lake for storing water) or to produce electricity
words.example: the Narmada dam in India

words.def: causing damage; having a bad effect on somebody/something
words.example: damaging consequences/effects

words.def: the time of day when light first appears
words.example: at dawn, They start work at dawn.

words.def: pieces of wood, metal, building materials, etc. that are left after something has been destroyed
words.example: Emergency teams are still clearing the debris from the plane crash.

words.def: the first public appearance of a performer or sports player
words.example: He will make his debut for the first team this week.

words.def: the process of deciding about something important, especially in a group of people or in an organization
words.example: responsibility for decision-making

words.def: very important for the final result of a particular situation
words.example: a decisive factor/victory/battle

words.def: an official or formal statement, especially about the plans of a government or an organization; the act of making such a statement
words.example: to issue/sign a declaration

words.def: working hard at something because it is very important to you
words.example: a dedicated teacher

words.def: the hard work and effort that somebody puts into an activity or a purpose because they think it is important
words.example: hard work and dedication

words.def: a thing that somebody does that is usually very good or very bad
words.example: It's a stirring tale of heroic deeds.

words.def: to have a particular opinion about somebody/something
words.example: deem somebody/something + noun, The evening was deemed a great success.

words.def: what happens or appears if you do not make any other choice or change, especially in a computer program
words.example: The default is fifty lines.

words.def: a fault in something or in the way it has been made that means that it is not perfect
words.example: a defect in the glass

words.def: protecting somebody/something against attack
words.example: As a defensive measure he built a series of coastal forts and watchtowers.

words.def: the state of not having, or not having enough of, something that is essential
words.example: deficiency (in something), Vitamin deficiency in the diet can cause illness.

words.def: the amount by which money spent or owed is greater than money earned in a particular period of time
words.example: a budget/trade deficit

words.def: to refuse to obey or show respect for somebody in authority, a law, a rule, etc.
words.example: I wouldn't have dared to defy my teachers.

words.def: a person who is chosen or elected to represent the views of a group of people and vote and make decisions for them
words.example: Congress delegates rejected the proposals.

words.def: a group of people who represent the views of an organization, a country, etc.
words.example: the Dutch delegation to the United Nations

words.def: easily damaged or broken
words.example: delicate china teacups

words.def: an evil spirit
words.example: The people believed the girl was possessed by demons.

words.def: a statement that something is not true or does not exist; the action of denying something
words.example: denial (of something), the prisoner’s repeated denials of the charges against him

words.def: to strongly criticize somebody/something that you think is wrong, illegal, etc.
words.example: denounce somebody/something, She publicly denounced the government's handling of the crisis.

words.def: containing a lot of people, things, plants, etc. with little space between them
words.example: a dense crowd/forest

words.def: the quality of being dense; the degree to which something is dense
words.example: The population density in this city is very high.

words.def: the state of needing the help and support of somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
words.example: Our relationship was based on mutual dependence.

words.def: to show an image of somebody/something in a picture
words.example: depict somebody/something (as somebody/something), a painting depicting the Virgin and Child

words.def: to move soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action
words.example: 2 000 troops were deployed in the area.

words.def: the act of moving soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action
words.example: the deployment of peacekeeping forces

words.def: a sum of money that is given as the first part of a larger payment
words.example: They normally ask you to pay $100 (as a) deposit.

words.def: a person who is the next most important person below a business manager, a head of a school, a political leader, etc. and who does that person’s job when they are away
words.example: I'm acting as deputy till the manager returns.

words.def: to come or go down from a higher to a lower level
words.example: The plane began to descend.

words.def: an action of coming or going down
words.example: The plane began its descent to Heathrow.

words.def: to say officially that somebody/something has a particular character or name; to describe somebody/something in a particular way
words.example: be designated (as) something, This area has been designated (as) a National Park.

words.def: that you would like to have or do; worth having or doing
words.example: She chatted for a few minutes about the qualities she considered desirable in a secretary.

words.def: a screen on a computer that shows the icons of programs and files that can be used
words.example: desktop icons

words.def: causing destruction or damage
words.example: The war demonstrated the destructive power of modern weapons.

words.def: to keep somebody in an official place, such as a police station, a prison or a hospital, and prevent them from leaving
words.example: One man has been detained for questioning.
